content: PostgreSQL sequences (which back Prisma's autoincrement()) are never rolled back on transaction abort by design (performance tradeoff) -- nextval() consumption is permanent even if the surrounding INSERT/transaction rolls back. This means poNumber may have gaps (e.g. PO-0001, PO-0003 if PO-0002's creation failed/rolled back) but this does NOT corrupt data -- gaps are cosmetic only, never duplicate or corrupt. Plan should document this as expected, not a bug.
